question: Compare the NuScale module description from Ch.01 Figure 1.2-5 with the Ch.05 SG parameters in Table 5.4-1 to understand the integrated design.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details the integration of these components within the RPV, highlights the boundaries between primary and secondary systems, and references where further design details can be found in other report sections.

nuscale_ch01_0032.title: Radioactive Waste Management System
nuscale_ch01_0032.summary: The section describes the radioactive waste management system at the NuScale Power Plant, noting that liquid, gaseous, and solid waste systems are detailed in Chapter 11, along with effluent monitoring. It also outlines the general arrangement of major structures, focusing on the centrally located, reinforced concrete Reactor Building (RXB), which houses the nuclear power modules and is designed for safety, radiation protection, and operational needs.

nuscale_ch01_0769.title: SRP 15.6.1, Rev 2: Inadvertent Opening of a PWR Pressurizer Relief Valve or a BWR Pressure Relief ValveAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0769.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 15.6.1, which addresses the inadvertent opening of pressurizer relief valves, because it does not use power-operated relief valves (PORVs) that are susceptible to such failures. Instead, NuScale uses spring-loaded safety relief valves, and any mechanical failure is considered bounded by analyses of inadvertent ECCS valve actuation. Thus, the specific guidance for PORVs is not fully applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, plenum access ports, and thermal relief valves, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the SGs and contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central placement and design of the Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and chapters for further information on radioactive waste management and building layouts.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance across its design power range. Key operational parameters—such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures—are optimized to maximize electrical output, ensure efficient use of support systems, and maintain safety margins, with calculations performed using NRELAP5 software and verified manually. The analysis accounts for uncertainties in flow resistance and system design, confirming that the reactor’s natural circulation is supported by low pressure losses and that certain uncertainties, like SG tube plugging, do not affect primary coolant flow.

nuscale_ch01_0407.title: SRP 9.5.3, Rev 3: Lighting SystemsII.1 Integrated Design of the System
nuscale_ch01_0407.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with regulatory criteria for lighting and communication systems, noting that the integrated design, emergency lighting, and lighting levels all conform to SRP 9.5.3 requirements. Site-specific and programmatic aspects, particularly for physical security communication systems, are the responsibility of the COL applicant, while the certified design addresses physical design elements. Some requirements for physical protection and communication fall partially under the COL applicant and are addressed in the facility’s security plan.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, system components, and safety systems, showing that NuScale uses a smaller, simpler, and more integrated design with fewer external components compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for clarity.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information alongside written descriptions. It references tables and figures that list and explain the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides an overview of the NuScale plant design, summarizing key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, noting that some information is representative or conceptual and marked accordingly. The section also references figures illustrating the plant layout and system boundaries.

nuscale_ch01_0599.title: SRP 13.3, Rev 3: Emergency PlanningII.20 Complete and Integrated Emergency Plans Associated with Early Site Permit Application
nuscale_ch01_0599.summary: This section outlines NuScale's conformance with emergency planning regulatory criteria, noting that most requirements are not applicable to the Combined License (COL) applicant and instead pertain to early site permit applicants. The only partially applicable criterion is the inclusion of an emergency response data system in the NuScale design, with site-specific responsibilities left to the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a crucial barrier to fission product release and supports various reactor components, with its materials and fabrication adhering to strict ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity and safety.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It lists the relevant diagrams, legends, and symbols used, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and secondary side corrosion risks considered low.

nuscale_ch01_0067.title: Drawings and Other Detailed Information
nuscale_ch01_0067.summary: This section describes the inclusion of simplified instrumentation, control, electrical, and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside written descriptions. It lists the types of diagrams provided, references tables and figures for diagram legends, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must supply site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the types of steel and nickel-based alloys used, their protective claddings, and the relevant material standards from the BPVC Section II to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes special plant features of the NuScale Power Plant, highlighting structures such as the Central Utility Building and Diesel Generator Buildings, which house essential but nonsafety-related equipment and functions. It also emphasizes human factors engineering, noting that the plant design minimizes human error through automation, digital controls, and optimized workload management, in compliance with regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section A provides detailed design information about the integrated components within the NuScale module, while Section B offers a general plant layout that visually contextualizes where these components, including those described in Section A, are situated within the overall facility.

Section A describes the integrated components of the NuScale module, including the steam generators and pressurizer, while Section B lists regulatory conformance for system malfunctions involving these same components, connecting the physical design to its regulatory evaluation.

Section A provides a general description of the NuScale module's integrated components, including the steam generators, while Section B (Table 5.4-1) specifies the detailed design parameters for those steam generators, connecting the overall system description to specific component values.

Section A provides detailed descriptions of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, which are depicted in the general arrangement overview referenced in Section B, thereby connecting specific subsystem design details to their physical placement in the plant layout.

Section A provides the detailed physical configuration and components of the NuScale module, including the steam generators (SGs), while Section B discusses the operational evaluation and performance criteria of the system, referencing the SGs and indicating that their detailed description is found in Section 5.4, thus connecting the physical design to its operational assessment.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant layout and references where detailed system descriptions can be found, while Section B specifies the internal components of the NuScale Power Module, including the steam generators, thereby detailing part of the integrated design referenced in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview and references detailed descriptions of plant systems, while Section B discusses system evaluation criteria and references specific sections (like 5.4) for detailed SG parameters, thus both sections structurally reference other parts of the FSAR for detailed information relevant to the integrated design.

Section B provides the detailed design and parameters of the NuScale module's steam generators, which are referenced in Section A's analysis of system malfunctions and conformance with regulatory criteria, connecting the physical design (B) to the regulatory evaluation (A).

Section B provides the general arrangement and layout of major structures and equipment, which includes the NuScale module described in Section A, thereby offering the physical context for the design and operational parameters referenced in Section A.

Section B provides specific system evaluation and design parameter details for the NuScale steam generators (SGs), which are referenced in Section A's regulatory conformance discussion to demonstrate that the design meets the regulatory requirements.

Section A provides detailed design information about the steam generators within the NuScale module, while Section B offers a general layout of the plant where these steam generators are located, connecting the specific component parameters to their placement in the overall plant arrangement.

Section A provides the detailed physical description and components of the NuScale module, including the steam generators, while Section B discusses the system-level evaluation and performance criteria that rely on the parameters and configuration described in Section A.

Section A provides the physical layout and arrangement of major structures, including the steam generators (SGs), while Section B details the operational parameters and performance criteria for the SGs, connecting the physical design to its functional evaluation.

Section A provides the integrated design context for the NuScale module, while Section B details the specific components and parameters (such as the steam generators) that realize this integrated design, connecting the general system overview to its concrete subsystem specifications.

Section A provides regulatory criteria and conformance statements for integrated system design, while Section B describes the physical arrangement and details of the radioactive waste management system, with both sections connected through their focus on how integrated design addresses regulatory requirements for plant systems.

Section A and Section B both reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report and its conformance with regulatory criteria, connecting through their reliance on certified design parameters and compliance with DSRS requirements for different systems (lighting/communication vs. chemical/volume control), thus illustrating how integrated design and regulatory conformance are addressed across different safety systems.

Section A provides a high-level comparison of NuScale module features and parameters (including those depicted in Figure 1.2-5), while Section B details the specific design and components (such as the steam generators) whose parameters are listed in Table 5.4-1, thus Section B specifies the technical details underlying the general descriptions in Section A.

Section A provides a comparison of NuScale's major design features and parameters with other facilities, while Section B describes the general arrangement and layout of the NuScale plant, with both sections referencing the plant's design features and parameters to give context to the integrated design.
Section A provides nominal design parameters for the NuScale module, which serve as input values or context for the regulatory conformance assessments described in Section B.

Section A provides electrical and instrumentation drawings that visually clarify the design and component positions of the NuScale module, which supports the detailed component and subsystem descriptions (including steam generator parameters) found in Section B.

Section A provides detailed drawings and visual clarifications of the NuScale module's electrical and I&C systems, which are part of the overall plant layout described in Section B, connecting the specific system details to the general arrangement of major structures and equipment.

Section A provides detailed electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify the NuScale module's design, which supports the regulatory conformance claims in Section B by serving as evidence for how the design meets input parameter and initial condition requirements.

Section A provides a high-level overview of the NuScale module and references the presence of major systems, while Section B specifies detailed design parameters and components (such as the steam generators) that are summarized in Section A, thus elaborating on the general description with concrete technical details.

Section A provides a high-level overview of the plant's systems, including the radioactive waste management system, while Section B elaborates specifically on the radioactive waste management system referenced in Section A, detailing its components and cross-referencing further sections for in-depth discussion.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale plant design, which establishes the context and system boundaries necessary to interpret the specific safety analysis conformance statements and input parameters listed in Section B.

Section B provides the detailed design and parameters of the NuScale module's reactor coolant system and steam generators, which are referenced in Section A's emergency planning context as part of the integrated system requiring evaluation for regulatory conformance.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale module's integrated components, including the steam generators, while Section B specifies the detailed design and arrangement of these components within the reactor pressure vessel, connecting the module description to specific SG parameters.

Section A describes the integrated components of the NuScale module, including the reactor vessel and steam generators, while Section B outlines the general arrangement and supporting systems such as radioactive waste management, both contributing to an understanding of the plant's integrated design.

Section A describes the physical configuration and components of the NuScale Power Module, including the reactor vessel and steam generators, while Section B lists regulatory conformance statements for system malfunctions, both referencing the integrated design and operational parameters of the module.

Section A provides regulatory criteria and integrated design requirements for the NuScale system, while Section B presents a comparison of NuScale's design features and parameters with those of typical PWRs, connecting them through the use of design values to demonstrate compliance and context for regulatory review.

Section A outlines the integrated design approach and regulatory conformance for systems like lighting and communication, while Section B provides the supporting electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify and detail these integrated system designs.

Section A specifies the integrated design approach and regulatory conformance for lighting and communication systems, while Section B provides a general summary of the plant's systems, including those detailed in Section A, thus Section A provides detailed specifications for the general descriptions in Section B.

Section A discusses integrated design aspects of the NuScale module relevant to physical security and communication systems, while Section B addresses emergency planning requirements that are designated as the responsibility of the COL applicant, indicating that understanding the integrated design in Section A is a prerequisite for the emergency planning considerations in Section B.

Section A provides an overview of the integrated design approach for the NuScale module, while Section B details the specific components and configuration of the reactor vessel, with both sections connected through their focus on the integrated design of the NuScale Power Module as referenced in the analysis question.
Section A provides nominal design parameters for the NuScale module, which are further clarified and visually represented by the detailed drawings and figures referenced in Section B.

Section A provides a comparative overview of NuScale's design features and parameters, referencing nominal values that are further detailed in the general plant description found in Section B, which outlines the overall facility and its systems.

Section A provides nominal design parameters and features for the NuScale module, which are further detailed and specified in Section B's description of the reactor vessel and its integrated components, including the steam generators.

Section A provides detailed electrical and I&C drawings that visually clarify and specify the general plant systems described in Section B's overall facility summary, directly connecting specific component layouts to the broader system descriptions.

Section A provides electrical and instrumentation drawings that visually clarify the integrated design described in Section B, which details the physical configuration and components (including steam generators) of the NuScale Power Module.

Section A provides a general description of the plant's design and systems, which forms the foundational context for Section B's discussion of emergency planning criteria and regulatory conformance, as understanding the plant's design is a prerequisite for evaluating emergency planning requirements.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale plant and references the integrated systems, while Section B specifies detailed components of the reactor vessel, including the steam generators mentioned in the general description.

Section A provides visual and schematic representations of the NuScale module's systems, which clarify and detail the integrated component and subsystem descriptions—including the steam generator (SG) parameters—presented in Section B.
Section A provides detailed drawings and diagrams that visually clarify the design and arrangement of plant systems, which support the general plant layout and system descriptions found in Section B.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the NuScale module's design, which are referenced in Section B as part of demonstrating conformance with regulatory criteria for system malfunctions and safety analysis.

Section A discusses specific operational experience and design considerations for steam generator (SG) tubing, while Section B provides a general overview of the integrated NuScale Power Module (NPM) components, including the SGs, thus Section A specifies details for the general system described in Section B.

Section A discusses the design and operational considerations of the steam generator (SG) tubing in the reactor coolant system, while Section B provides a general overview and references the layout of major plant systems, including the radioactive waste management system, thus Section B provides the broader context in which the specific SG design details from Section A are situated.

Section A discusses the design and operational experience of the NuScale steam generator (SG) tubing, while Section B lists regulatory conformance statements for initial conditions and input parameters, indicating that the SG design features described in Section A are intended to satisfy the regulatory requirements referenced in Section B.

Section A provides visual and schematic details of the NuScale module's systems, which contextualize and clarify the specific steam generator (SG) design parameters and operational considerations described in Section B.

Section A provides visual and schematic details of the NuScale module, including figures like Ch.01 Figure 1.2-5, which clarify the physical arrangement and integration of components whose specific parameters (such as those for the steam generators) are detailed in Section B's Table 5.4-1, thus connecting the general module layout to the technical specifications of its subsystems.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design of NuScale modules, which are referenced in Section B's general plant description to illustrate the arrangement of major structures and equipment, thereby connecting visual design information to the overall system layout.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures of the NuScale module, which visually clarify the integrated system design that is further quantified by the specific steam generator (SG) parameters listed in Section B's Table 5.4-1, thus connecting visual design representation with technical parameter specification.
Section A provides detailed material specifications for the pressurizer components that are generally described as part of the integrated reactor pressure vessel and its subsystems in Section B.

Section A details the materials and components of the pressurizer within the reactor coolant system, while Section B provides a general overview and layout of the plant including references to the radioactive waste management system, with both sections referencing different subsystems that are physically and functionally integrated within the overall NuScale module as depicted in the plant layout figures.

Section A provides detailed descriptions and material specifications for pressurizer components referenced in the NuScale module, while Section B documents the conformance of the NuScale design (including these components) with regulatory criteria for system malfunctions.

Section A provides a general description of plant structures and utility systems, while Section B specifies the detailed design and components of the reactor coolant system, with both sections connected through their descriptions of integrated plant systems that support the NuScale module's operation.

Section A describes the Central Utility Building and its utility functions, while Section B outlines the general arrangement of major plant structures including the radioactive waste management system, with both sections providing complementary details about plant layout and support systems relevant to understanding the integrated design.

Section A describes the physical plant features and utility systems (such as chemical treatment equipment for demineralized water) that support reactor operations, while Section B references regulatory conformance for systems (like the Chemical and Volume Control System) that rely on those plant features for safe operation, thus connecting the physical infrastructure to regulatory compliance.

Section A provides visual and schematic details of NuScale module components, which are referenced in Section B's textual description of specific Reactor Coolant System components and their materials, connecting the visual design with the detailed component specifications.

Section A provides detailed drawings and figures that visually clarify the design and layout of plant systems, which can include the Central Utility Building and its utility services described in Section B, thereby specifying the physical arrangement for the general features listed in Section B.

Section A details the materials and components of the pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B describes the Central Utility Building and its housed systems, with both sections contributing to an integrated understanding of the plant's overall design by specifying different but complementary subsystems.
